title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. luxurians
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. luxurians, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. luxurians include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its therapeutic properties.
